title: Chapter 2: First Feed
content: She took the saltines.

Marcus stood frozen in his doorway, arm still extended, watching the most beautiful corpse he'd ever seen cradle a sleeve of crackers like they were made of glass. Her grey fingers—long, elegant, perfectly manicured despite the end of civilization—tore the plastic with surgical precision.
She didn't eat them. She held one to her nose and inhaled.
Then she looked at him.
Those grey eyes had something in them. Not human—not anymore—but not empty either. Like a computer in sleep mode, screen dark but power light still glowing.

**[SERAPHINA VALE is examining your offering.]**
**[Assessment: Low nutritional value. Insufficient for evolution.]**
**[But she appreciates the gesture.]**
**[Affection: 3 → 5/100]**
"That's it? Two points for saltines?" Marcus whispered. "They were sea salt—"

Seraphina placed a single cracker on her tongue. Her jaw moved slowly, mechanically, like someone relearning how to chew. A crumb fell from her lip. She caught it with two fingers and placed it back in her mouth.
For a zombie, she had remarkably good table manners.
**[System Reward Delivered: 3x Fresh Bread Rolls, 1x Bottled Water, 1x System Strawberry]**

The items materialized on his kitchen counter. No fanfare, no light show—one second the counter was bare, the next it wasn't. Three golden-brown rolls still warm to the touch. A water bottle with condensation beading down the sides. And a strawberry so red it looked painted, plump and perfect in a world where fruit was a memory.
Marcus grabbed a bread roll. Bit into it.
His eyes watered.

Eight days of instant ramen and fear. Eight days of watching his world shrink to the dimensions of a one-bedroom apartment. And now—warm bread. Actual bread. With a golden crust that crackled between his teeth and a soft interior that tasted like every breakfast he'd ever skipped to sleep in.
He ate the second roll without coming up for air.

The third, he wrapped carefully and set aside. Rationing. Always rationing. Even when magic appeared in his kitchen, the survival instinct his gaming sessions had somehow forged wasn't going anywhere.
A sound from the doorway. A low, resonant click from Seraphina's throat.
She was staring at the strawberry.
"System," Marcus muttered, "what happens if I feed her the strawberry?"

**[System Strawberry: Rare-tier feed item.]**
**[When consumed by a zombie with consciousness potential:]**
**[- Restores partial cognitive function for 1-2 hours]**
**[- Kickstarts Stage 1 Evolution]**
**[- May trigger emotional imprinting on the provider]**
**[Warning: Emotional imprinting creates a bond. You will become her anchor.]**
"Her anchor."

**[She will imprint on you as her primary emotional reference point. You will be the fulcrum of her returning consciousness.]**
**[In practical terms: she will not harm you. She may protect you. She will follow you.]**
**[This bond is not reversible.]**
So he'd be stuck with a zombie following him around. A zombie who might or might not decide to eat his face depending on how she felt about him.
On the other hand: he'd have a zombie following him around. One powerful enough to—what, exactly? He didn't know. The System said "evolution," but evolution into what?
He picked up the strawberry. Held it out.
Seraphina's nostrils flared. Her whole body oriented toward the fruit like a compass needle finding north. A sound escaped her—not a growl, not a groan, but something softer. A keen. Like a wolf pup.

She took a step toward him. Then another. Close enough that he could smell her—rot and copper and, underneath it, something that might have been perfume. Something floral and expensive, barely clinging to skin that no longer sweat.
Her fingers closed around the strawberry. Their hands touched.
Her skin was cold. Really cold—like touching a window in January. But not slimy, not decayed. Smooth and firm, preserved like a specimen in formaldehyde.
She bit into the strawberry.

**[SERAPHINA VALE consuming System Strawberry...]**
**[Evolution Initiating...]**
**[Stage 0 (Mindless) → Stage 1 (Aware)]**

The change wasn't dramatic. No transformation sequence, no light pillar, no level-up fanfare. But Marcus saw it: her eyes. The grey didn't leave, but it was joined by something else—a pupil that dilated. Focused. Actually *looked* at him instead of through him.
Her lips were stained red from the strawberry juice. She licked them—a human gesture, slow and almost deliberate.
Then she spoke.
"...good."
One word. Barely a whisper, barely formed, the consonants dragging like a skipping record. But a word. A real, intentional, meaningful word.

**[AFFECTION: 5 → 15/100]**
**[Emotional Imprinting: COMPLETE]**
**[Seraphina Vale is now bonded to you.]**
**[She will not harm you. She will protect you to the extent of her current abilities.]**
**[Current Abilities: Enhanced speed, Enhanced strength, Preserved human cognition (limited)]**
Marcus let out a breath he hadn't realized he'd been holding. He'd just fed a zombie. He'd just *talked* to a zombie. And the zombie had talked back.
"Good," he repeated, and something like a laugh bubbled up from his chest. "Yeah. It was good."
Seraphina tilted her head. The gesture was almost... curious. Almost human.

Then she walked past him into his apartment, sat down on his couch—in the exact spot he always sat, the one with the permanent ass-groove in the cushion—and stared at his dead monitor as if expecting it to turn on.
Marcus stood in the doorway, the hallway's carnage stench washing over him, and looked at the zombie woman making herself comfortable in his living room.
"This is insane," he told no one.

**[NEW QUEST AVAILABLE]**
**[QUEST: Close Quarters]**
**[Objective: Have Seraphina stay in your apartment for 12 consecutive hours.]**
**[Condition: You must sleep in the same room as her.]**
**[Risk Assessment: She is bonded to you. The risk is not from her—it's from what follows the scent of System food.]**
**[Reward: Reinforced Door Upgrade, 2x Canned Meals, 1x Premium Feed Item (Random)]**
**[Time Limit: 12 hours from acceptance]**
**[Accept? Y/N]**
Twelve hours with a zombie. Sleeping in the same room. In an apartment with a door that wouldn't survive another Mrs. Patterson assault.
On the other hand: reinforced door. Canned meals. More food for Sera.
"Sera," he said, trying out the nickname. "That work for you?"
She turned from the monitor. Looked at him with those pupils that shouldn't be able to focus but did.
"...Sera."
She said it back. Like a name she was trying on. Like it mattered.
Marcus closed the door.

**[QUEST ACCEPTED: Close Quarters]**
**[Timer: 11:59:58]**
Outside, something howled. Not Sera. Something else. Something that had caught the scent of system-spawned bread and strawberry and was very, very interested.
He had twelve hours. Twelve hours to survive the night with a zombie girlfriend he'd known for fifteen minutes, in an apartment surrounded by things that wanted to eat them both.
Marcus looked at Seraphina. She was examining a half-empty Monster Energy can on the coffee table, sniffing it with the focused intensity of a sommelier.
Worst date of his life. Best date of his life. Only date of his life.
He accepted the quest.
